question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Short answer - Most likely root cause: an explicit “Deny” permission (an ACE) on the printer queue for that specific user. That produces an immediate “Access denied” before any job reaches the queue, and was the root cause in a matching incident from Basel

context: SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

context: SOURCE 2: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default Ticket number: TI-1623017 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 18 Owner: Employee 23 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.11.25 08:49 Closure Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11 Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable. Activities 18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18 Good morning Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location. They must manually select the correct printer before each print job. The issue seems to have started last week. Kind regards 18.11.25 08:49 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23. 18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with an affected user. Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”. Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list. Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set. 18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department? Kind regards 18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week. The old group was deactivated. 19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that: • Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups • Default printers per location are set via GPO • No parallel groups with identical function may exist Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group. As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default. 19.11.25 11:30 – Test Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership. gpupdate /force executed. User logged in again. Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”. 20.11.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization. Correction performed according to blueprint specification. 24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback Multiple users tested. Default printer remains correct. No further automatic switching. 24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 18 The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment. Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default. context: SOURCE 3: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue Ticket number: TI-1623488 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 20 Owner: Employee 27 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 02.12.25 09:03 Closure Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21 Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again. Activities 02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents. All other employees in the same room can print without issue. The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print. 02.12.25 09:03 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27. 02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with the affected user. Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible. When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately. Print job is not displayed in the queue. 02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user Test performed with colleague in the same office. Printing works without restriction. User account checked. Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed. 02.12.25 10:30 – Further review Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. It specifies: • Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups • No individual user permissions at queue level • NTFS and share permissions must be consistent Review of the printer queue on the print server shows: • AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission • Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny” This entry originates from a previous test configuration. 02.12.25 11:10 – Test Explicit deny permission removed. User logged in again. Test print successful. 03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback Printing works normally again. Additional tests within the team successful. 03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 20 The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user. The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. Printing is now fully functional again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.

context: SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. context: SOURCE 2: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default Ticket number: TI-1623017 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 18 Owner: Employee 23 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.11.25 08:49 Closure Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11 Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable. Activities 18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18 Good morning Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location. They must manually select the correct printer before each print job. The issue seems to have started last week. Kind regards 18.11.25 08:49 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23. 18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with an affected user. Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”. Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list. Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set. 18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department? Kind regards 18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week. The old group was deactivated. 19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that: • Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups • Default printers per location are set via GPO • No parallel groups with identical function may exist Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group. As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default. 19.11.25 11:30 – Test Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership. gpupdate /force executed. User logged in again. Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”. 20.11.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization. Correction performed according to blueprint specification. 24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback Multiple users tested. Default printer remains correct. No further automatic switching. 24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 18 The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment. Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default. Scan-to-Mail fully restored. Activities 10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25 Good morning At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails. Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally. Printing continues to function without issue. Could you please check this? Kind regards 10.12.25 08:41 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30. 10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis Test scan to internal mailbox → successful. Test scan to external address → no delivery. No error message visible on the printer itself. 10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network? 10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted. Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location. 11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis Internal delivery occurs directly. External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay. SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”. 11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0” Section Scan-to-Mail: • Printers must not send directly to the internet • Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay • Relay allows only defined IP ranges • DNS resolution must occur internally • Authentication must not be bypassed 12.12.25 – Further analysis Check of printer IP address. Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN. This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay. Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked. 15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list. 16.12.25 – Adjustment SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint. IP range of printer VLAN released. 17.12.25 – Test phase Scan to internal mailbox → successful Scan to external Gmail address → successful Scan to partner company → successful 18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 25 The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients. question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . What to include in the ticket to speed resolution - A short reproduction: user name, time of test, exact error text (“Access denied”), apps tested (Word/Outlook/Browser), and that reboot/remove/re-add were tried. - Confirmation of AD group membership (current groups) and recent group changes from HR/IT (if known). - A screenshot of the error and the user’s Printers & Scanners view. - If available, a remote session log or print‑server event/log entries showing denial (or absence) of the job. - Who is currently the owner of the print-server or AD group (to route escalation). Recommended immediate action (if you can do it now) - L1: remote into the user, confirm group membership and gpupdate, then have the print-server admin check and remove any explicit “Deny” ACE on the queue and ask the user to re-login and test. This sequence resolved the identical Basel incident

Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.

Behavior stable. Activities 18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18 Good morning Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location. They must manually select the correct printer before each print job. The issue seems to have started last week. Kind regards 18.11.25 08:49 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23. 18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with an affected user. Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”. Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list. Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set. 18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department? Kind regards 18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week. context: SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company
